reasoning: The Lean statement encodes "there exists a unique pair (a, n) of positive naturals with a^(n+1) - (a+1)^n = 2001" using ∃! over ℕ × ℕ, with p.1 > 0 and p.2 > 0 corresponding to "positive integers." Although ℕ uses truncated subtraction, this does not introduce spurious solutions: if (a+1)^n > a^(n+1), the truncated result is 0 ≠ 2001; otherwise the ℕ subtraction equals the ℤ subtraction. Hence the solution set of pairs is identical to the mathematical one, and the uniqueness claim matches the informal statement exactly. No extra hypotheses are added or dropped.
